title: Investments: compounding green numbers
subtitle: A short visualisation for building the felt sense of investments that grow quietly over time.
excerpt: A five-minute visualisation from Day 40 of the MONEY program. The scene: an investment account, a line that keeps climbing, and the calm of watching it without needing to act.
sourceNotes: Original to homemade.education. Creative visualisation as a self-development tool draws on public-domain traditions.

practice.whenToUse: Use to build the felt sense of watching investments compound before the account reflects it.

content.text: A five-minute visualisation from the Day 40 theme. The image is simple: a line chart, a balance, and you calm enough to watch both.
Sit or lie down. Close your eyes. One slow breath.
Picture an investment account open on your screen. The balance is shown in green. You don't need to know the exact number — just that it is positive and growing.

Now picture the line chart below the balance. It runs from left to right, and the line moves upward. Not in a straight line: there are small drops, small recoveries, but the overall direction is up. You watch it the way you would watch a plant growing — without needing to intervene.
Six months pass. The line is higher. A year. Still higher. You did not stress over the small drops. You let the compounding run.
Notice how that feels in the body: calm, not vigilant. The money is working. You are not watching it every day. It doesn't need you to.
Hold that image for two minutes. Then open your eyes.
Original to homemade.education. Creative visualisation as a self-development tool draws on public-domain traditions.
